subject: Public Health
title: Smallpox and the Fall of the Aztecs
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Smallpox and the Fall of the Aztecs.

hook.text: Spanish soldiers didn't conquer the Aztec Empire. Smallpox did most of the killing for them.

buildup.text: Indigenous peoples had no immunity to European diseases—they had never encountered them before.

discovery.text: Smallpox killed an estimated 90% of the Indigenous population of the Americas within a century.

twist.text: The devastation was so total that European colonizers assumed the land was 'empty' and unsettled.

climax.text: Disease—not military superiority—was the decisive weapon of colonization across the Americas.

punchline.text: A virus conquered more territory than any army.

quiz.question: What percentage of Indigenous Americans died from European diseases?
